metadata.title: StealthTalk - Alert to Microsoft Teams
metadata.description: When a Microsoft Sentinel incident is created, post a formatted Adaptive Card with the incident summary into a Microsoft Teams channel via a Power Automate Workflow webhook.
prerequisites: A Microsoft Teams channel with a Workflows incoming webhook ('When a Teams Webhook request is received' followed by 'Post card in a chat or channel') already configured. The webhook URL is provided as a deployment parameter.
After deployment, grant the Logic App's system-assigned managed identity the **Microsoft Sentinel Responder** role on the Microsoft Sentinel workspace.
Grant the **Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor** role to the Azure Security Insights service principal on the Logic App resource group.
Create an Automation Rule in Microsoft Sentinel to run this playbook on incidents from the four StealthTalk analytic rules.
postDeployment: Grant the Logic App's system-assigned managed identity the **Microsoft Sentinel Responder** role on the Microsoft Sentinel workspace.
Grant the **Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor** role to the Azure Security Insights service principal on the Logic App resource group.

Create a Microsoft Sentinel **Automation Rule** that runs this playbook on incidents from the four StealthTalk analytic rules: `StealthTalk - After hours work`, `StealthTalk - Multi new devices registration`, `StealthTalk - Login outside work zone`, `StealthTalk - Password brute force`.
Configure the **TeamsWebhookUrl** parameter with the Workflows incoming-webhook URL from the target Teams channel.

releaseNotes.title: Initial release
notes: Initial release of the StealthTalk Alert-to-Teams playbook.
Microsoft Sentinel incident trigger; Adaptive Card posted to Teams via Power Automate Workflows incoming webhook.

metadata.description: Name of the Logic App / Playbook resource.

Power Automate / Teams Workflow incoming-webhook URL. Created in the target Teams channel under Workflows -> 'When a Teams Webhook request is received' -> 'Post card in a chat or channel'. Treated as a secret because the URL contains an embedded HMAC signature.
